articles.title: Is India really a team full of flat-track bullies? Why context matters when assessing T20I slump
articles.summary: India has suffered five consecutive T20I defeats during series held in Ireland and England. These losses have sparked a debate regarding whether the national side consists of "flat-track bullies." Jamie Alter of Cricket News provided an assessment of the slump, noting the importance of context when evaluating the team's performance.
articles.description: Five consecutive T20I defeats, spread across series in Ireland and England, have inevitably sparked a familiar debate: is this Indian side simply a team of flat-track bullies? The Cricket News' Jamie Alter gives his take...
articles.author: Jamie Alter

articles.title: 'Needs to be controlled!' - BCCI issues blunt warning to Abhishek Sharma and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after England series defeat
articles.summary: Following India's T20I series defeat against England in Bristol, a BCCI source issued a warning to openers Abhishek Sharma and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi regarding their batting style. The source stated that the players' approach "needs to be controlled" after the loss.
articles.description: Following India's T20I series defeat in Bristol, a BCCI source issued a big warning to openers Abhishek Sharma and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi over their batting style.

articles.title: Bayer Sells $3.4 Billion Contraceptives Stake to Apollo
articles.summary: Bayer AG sold a minority stake in its contraceptives business to Apollo Global Management Inc. for €3 billion ($3.4 billion). The company will use the proceeds from the sale to help cover rising litigation costs associated with the herbicide Roundup.

articles.title: Japan’s Producer Prices Pick Up to Fastest Pace Since Early 2023
articles.summary: Japan’s Producer Prices Pick Up to Fastest Pace Since Early 2023. Japan’s corporate goods prices picked up in June to the fastest pace since early 2023, adding to evidence of rising inflationary pressures that support the case for the Bank of Japan to keep raising interest rates.
